# FamHop

> FamHop is a free family weekend planner for parents and caregivers. Visitors choose a metro area, pick a vibe, and FamHop builds a 3-stop plan from kid-friendly parks, libraries, museums, zoos, farms, food stops, and upcoming family events.

The site is a JavaScript single-page app at https://famhop.com. Static SEO pages and machine-readable data are published for every supported metro so crawlers can understand the regional coverage without executing JavaScript.

## Coverage

FamHop currently covers:

- San Francisco Bay Area: https://famhop.com/bay-area/
- Los Angeles: https://famhop.com/los-angeles/
- New York City: https://famhop.com/new-york-city/
- Seattle: https://famhop.com/seattle/
- Chicago: https://famhop.com/chicago/
- Dallas-Fort Worth: https://famhop.com/dallas-fort-worth/
- Houston: https://famhop.com/houston/
- Washington DC: https://famhop.com/washington-dc/
- Atlanta: https://famhop.com/atlanta/
- Philadelphia: https://famhop.com/philadelphia/
- Miami: https://famhop.com/miami/
- Phoenix: https://famhop.com/phoenix/
- Boston: https://famhop.com/boston/
- San Diego: https://famhop.com/san-diego/

The root page, https://famhop.com/, links to all supported metro areas. Legacy aliases such as `/bayarea`, `/losangeles`, `/la`, `/nyc`, `/dfw`, `/dc`, `/philly`, and `/sandiego` are served with canonical tags pointing at the hyphenated metro URLs.

## URL Schema

All SEO pages are metro-prefixed:

- Metro home: `https://famhop.com/{metro}/`
- This weekend: `https://famhop.com/{metro}/this-weekend/`
- Place pages: `https://famhop.com/{metro}/spot/{slug}/`
- Event pages: `https://famhop.com/{metro}/event/{slug}/`
- City pages: `https://famhop.com/{metro}/city/{slug}/`
- Category pages: `https://famhop.com/{metro}/category/{library|museum|park|festival|zoo|farm|community}/`

Supported metro slugs are `bay-area`, `los-angeles`, `new-york-city`, `seattle`, `chicago`, `dallas-fort-worth`, `houston`, `washington-dc`, `atlanta`, `philadelphia`, `miami`, `phoenix`, `boston`, and `san-diego`.

## Key Data Files

The shared JSON data feed is public and CORS-enabled. Search engines should use the HTML SEO pages as indexable URLs; JSON files carry `X-Robots-Tag: noindex` to avoid duplicate indexing.

Per-metro data files are served under:

- Places: `https://famhop.com/data/{metro}/spots.json`
- Events: `https://famhop.com/data/{metro}/events.json`
- Featured plans: `https://famhop.com/data/{metro}/featured-plans.json`

Replace `{metro}` with any supported slug listed above.

Legacy Bay Area files remain available for compatibility:

- https://famhop.com/data/bay-area-spots.json
- https://famhop.com/data/events.json
- https://famhop.com/data/event-build-report.json

## Data Sources

Place data is sourced from OpenStreetMap (ODbL), with imagery and attribution from Wikimedia Commons and Wikidata where available. Events are pulled from official calendars and public feeds for libraries, parks, museums, zoos, theaters, family festivals, and selected ticketed sources.

Every place and event carries an `audiences` array with values such as `"kids"`, `"adults"`, or `"all"`. FamHop is the kids app and displays records tagged for kids or all audiences.

## Structured Data

Static pages include schema.org JSON-LD:

- Metro and collection pages use `CollectionPage`.
- Place pages use `Place`, `TouristAttraction`, `Restaurant`, or `LocalBusiness` depending on category.
- Event pages use `Event` with date, location, audience, organizer, and offer data when available.
- Breadcrumbs use `BreadcrumbList`.
- The root page includes a `WebSite`, `Organization`, and metro `ItemList`.

## Product Behavior

1. Choose a metro area.
2. Pick a vibe such as chill, adventure, museum day, library hop, outdoors, or food.
3. Optionally choose an age band and target date.
4. FamHop returns a 3-stop plan tuned to the selected metro.
5. Tap Share to create a vote link. Recipients can vote Yes / Maybe / Skip on each stop without signing up.

## What FamHop Does Not Do

- FamHop is not a ticketing or booking platform. Tickets and registration remain on each venue's official site.
- FamHop does not collect children's personal data. Sign-in is optional and only syncs saved places and plans for the signed-in user.
- FamHop does not guarantee event availability; users should confirm details with the linked official source before going.
